items.name: accordion

items.name: alert-dialog
items.description: A confirm modal: an interruptive prompt requiring a response (delete this? discard?). Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012, Wave 3), the role=alertdialog specialization of the dialog wire: open-state + title/description/labels + the destructive flag live in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the destructive action + cancel are real l:click buttons, not slots. Renders the dialog wire's overlay/panel structure directly (no nested live instance, no <lv-dialog> island). WAI-ARIA APG alertdialog (role=alertdialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby); the backdrop does NOT dismiss (only the explicit buttons do); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.

The open state is server-owned: fire `open` from a trigger to show the prompt, `confirm` (the destructive/primary action) or `cancel` to close. Do the real destructive work server-side by overriding `confirm()` (with authz) before it closes, or read the `confirmed` wire flag on the host after the round-trip. An alertdialog is interruptive: the backdrop does NOT dismiss it, only the explicit buttons do. Focus-trap + Escape: wire a small typed-TS module keyed off [data-lv-alert-dialog] that traps Tab within [data-lv-alert-dialog-panel] while not [hidden] and dispatches the `cancel` action on Escape; the open-state itself stays server (wire). items.name: breadcrumb
items.description: An accessible breadcrumb navigation trail: <nav aria-label> landmark wrapping an ordered list, real <a href> for non-current items, aria-current='page' on the current (last) item, separator aria-hidden (WAI-ARIA breadcrumb pattern). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 4): the trail is the server-rendered route, so there is no client state and no island. Replaces the <lv-breadcrumb> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import java.util.List @import java.util.Map <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui breadcrumb partial -- an accessible breadcrumb navigation trail. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial (ADR-0012 server-first pivot). The former island held no client state; the trail is a fact the SERVER already knows (the route being rendered), so the server emits the whole
. The Lit island is gone. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA breadcrumb pattern (the model the dropped island cited). A
landmark wraps an ordered
    -- the ordinal list communicates the root-to-current sequence to assistive tech. Each non-current item is a real (the browser navigates, middle-click + open-in-new-tab work, no JS); the LAST item is the current page, rendered as a non-link (you cannot navigate to where you already are). The separator glyph between items is aria-hidden so a screen reader does not announce it. This mirrors the navigation-menu + sidebar partials: real anchors, server-known active state, zero client. DATA SHAPE: items arrive as a typed List>, root-to-current, each map keyed `label` (required, the visible text) and `href` (optional, the navigation target). The last item is treated as current regardless of whether it carries an href. This is the same List> idiom the dashboard block uses, so it compiles against the bare jte runtime with no custom record type to ship. CSS COMPAT: keeps the `lv-breadcrumb*` class names the island used so any stylesheet that targeted them still applies; the partial is self-styling via token-driven utilities. Params: List> items the trail, root-to-current; each map: label (+ optional href). String separator = "/" the glyph between items (aria-hidden). String label = "Breadcrumb" aria-label for the
    landmark. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the
    . Usage: @@template.breadcrumb(items = java.util.List.of( java.util.Map.of("label", "Home", "href", "/"), java.util.Map.of("label", "Properties", "href", "/properties"), java.util.Map.of("label", "Via Roma 12"))) --%> @param List> items = java.util.List.of() @param String separator = "/" @param String label = "Breadcrumb" @param String cssClass = ""
      @for(var i = 0; i < items.size(); i++) !{var item = items.get(i);} !{var isCurrent = i == items.size() - 1;} !{var isFirst = i == 0;} !{var href = item.get("href");}
    1. @if(!isFirst) ${separator} @endif @if(isCurrent) ${item.getOrDefault("label", "")} @else ${item.getOrDefault("label", "")} @endif
      2. @endfor

items.name: calendar
items.description: A standalone, inline month-grid date picker primitive (WAI-ARIA APG grid pattern); month nav with Lucide chevrons, roving-tabindex keyboard navigation (arrows + Home/End + PageUp/Down), single or range selection, today/selected/disabled states, min/max bounds, locale-aware first-day-of-week.
items.docs: Register <lv-calendar> by importing components/ui/calendar.ts from your entry point. Set `mode` ('single' | 'range'). Bind `value`: in single mode an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD), in range mode 'start,end'. Optional `min` / `max` (ISO) bound the selectable range; set the `isDisabled` property to a `(iso) => boolean` predicate to disable individual days; `weekStart` (0=Sun, 1=Mon default) and `locale` control the header. Listen for `lv-change` to receive the new value string.

items.name: chart
items.description: A server-rendered static bar chart as inline SVG: one <rect> per typed @param data point, coloured by the --lv-color-chart-N tokens, with axis labels and a WAI-ARIA img role (aria-label + in-SVG <title> text alternative, per-bar aria-label). Zero JS, no canvas, no client charting lib, CSP-clean. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-chart> Lit island. An interactive chart (hover tooltip, zoom) is an escape-hatch typed-TS micro-enhancement over this SVG, not shipped.

files.content:
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui chart partial -- a SERVER-rendered static bar chart as inline SVG (ADR-0012).
  The bars, axis labels and accessible title are all emitted from typed @param data; there is
  NO JavaScript, NO <canvas>, NO client charting library. Replaces the dropped <lv-chart> Lit
  island, whose hover tooltip + lv-point event were the only client behaviour and are not shipped.

  TIER: PARTIAL (server-rendered SVG). A chart is a pure projection of a fixed dataset: given the
  values, the picture is fully determined server-side, so a client renderer added only the risk of
  the silent-projection bug (the exact class that motivated the pivot) for no decision the server
  could not make. The values arrive via @param from the controller's typed model and the partial
  emits declarative <svg> markup the browser draws with zero boot. INTERACTIVE charting (hover
  tooltips, zoom, live updates) is an ESCAPE-HATCH, not shipped: an adopter who truly needs it drops
  a typed-TS micro-enhancement over this SVG in their own frontend/src/ (the documented seam), the
  way gest keeps ht-calendar. lievit-ui ships no charting island.

  STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the simplest useful shape the island supported is the bar
  chart (its default type="bar"), and a bar is the one chart whose geometry is a trivial server
  computation (a rect per datum, height = value / max). shadcn's chart guidance and the WAI-ARIA
  img role agree: a static data graphic is a single role="img" with a text alternative, NOT a
  widget. So the partial is one <svg role="img"> with an <title>/aria-label for the accessible
  name, one <rect> per data point coloured by the --lv-color-chart-N tokens (the same token cascade
  the island used), a baseline, and per-bar category labels under the plot. Native SVG beats a
  bundled lib on every axis here: a11y (the title is the text alternative a screen reader reads),
  zero dependency, and SSR-native declarative markup.

  A11y (WAI-ARIA img): the <svg> carries role="img" + aria-label (the chart's accessible name) and
  an in-SVG <title> as the first child (the SVG-native text alternative). Each bar also carries an
  aria-label="<category>: <value>" so the individual data is reachable; the decorative axis text is
  aria-hidden so the value is not announced twice.

  Params:
    String   label                accessible name for the whole chart (role=img aria-label + <title>).
    String[] categories           x-axis labels, one per data point.
    int[]    values               bar values, aligned positionally with categories.
    String   color    = "var(--lv-color-chart-1)"  bar fill; defaults to the chart-1 token.
    int      width    = 600       SVG viewBox width.
    int      height   = 300       SVG viewBox height.
    boolean  showAxis = true      render the category labels under the plot.
    String   cssClass = ""        extra utility classes on the <svg>.

  Usage:
    @@template.chart(label = "Listings closed", categories = months, values = closed)
    @@template.chart(label = "Leads", categories = sources, values = leads, color = "var(--lv-color-chart-3)")
--%>
@param String label
@param String[] categories
@param int[] values
@param String color = "var(--lv-color-chart-1)"
@param int width = 600
@param int height = 300
@param boolean showAxis = true
@param String cssClass = ""

!{
  int padTop = 12;
  int padRight = 12;
  int padBottom = showAxis ? 28 : 12;
  int padLeft = 36;
  int plotW = width - padLeft - padRight;
  int plotH = height - padTop - padBottom;
  int n = Math.max(values.length, 1);
  double bandW = (double) plotW / n;
  double barW = bandW * 0.7;
  int max = 1;
  for (int v : values) if (v > max) max = v;
}

<svg
  data-slot="chart"
  role="img"
  aria-label="${label}"
  viewBox="0 0 ${width} ${height}"
  preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet"
  class="block h-auto w-full ${cssClass}"
  style="font-family:var(--lv-font-sans);overflow:visible;"
>
  <title>${label}</title>

  <%-- baseline --%>
  !{int baseY = padTop + plotH;}
  <line x1="${padLeft}" y1="${baseY}" x2="${width - padRight}" y2="${baseY}" stroke="var(--lv-color-border)" stroke-width="1" />

  @for(int i = 0; i < values.length; i++)
    !{int v = values[i];}
    !{double bandX = padLeft + bandW * i;}
    !{int x = (int) Math.round(bandX + (bandW - barW) / 2.0);}
    !{int barH = (int) Math.round(plotH * ((double) v / max));}
    !{int y = baseY - barH;}
    !{int bw = (int) Math.round(barW);}
    !{String cat = i < categories.length ? categories[i] : String.valueOf(i);}
    <rect
      data-slot="chart-bar"
      x="${x}" y="${y}" width="${Math.max(bw, 1)}" height="${Math.max(barH, 0)}"
      rx="2"
      fill="${color}"
      role="img"
      aria-label="${cat}: ${v}"
    ></rect>
    @if(showAxis)
      !{int labelX = (int) Math.round(bandX + bandW / 2.0);}
      <text
        x="${labelX}" y="${height - 8}"
        text-anchor="middle"
        aria-hidden="true"
        fill="var(--lv-color-muted)"
        style="font-size:11px;"
      >${cat}</text>
    @endif
  @endfor
</svg>

items.name: data-table
items.description: A server-sorted, server-paginated data table: a real <table> whose sort and page are SERVER facts (sortable headers are real <a href> re-sort links carrying aria-sort, paging via the pagination partial). Rows come in via a Content slot (caller owns the cells). Empty state. Zero JS, zero client sort, CSP-clean. The presentational primitive the kit's admin list composes.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: data-table (static JTE partial - server-sorted/paginated table) The SERVER-FIRST data table: a real whose sort and page are SERVER facts. Sortable column headers are real re-sort links (the server produces the next sort URL per column); clicking one re-sorts on the server and re-renders. Pagination is the windowed pagination partial (real page links). The empty state renders a single spanning row when there are no rows. ZERO client JavaScript, ZERO client sort. TIER (why this is a partial, not a Lit island / wire component): the genuinely STATEFUL server-sorted/paginated table is OWNED by lievit-kit's admin list -- the ListingListComponent wire holds the page/sort/search/filter as @Wire fields, AdminListView.of(...) reads one bounded page, and l:click="toggleSort/prev/next" actions re-render it. This partial is NOT that engine; it is the presentational PRIMITIVE the kit (and any plain Spring controller) composes to RENDER one page. The kit's listing-list.jte hand-rolls this exact
///pagination markup; this partial generalises it so the markup, the aria-sort contract, and the sort-link affordance live in one owned place instead of being copy-pasted per resource. The old Lit island (client sort + client pagination, fully in the browser) is removed by ADR-0012: the table is server-first, the kit holds the state, this partial draws it. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule). shadcn's Data Table is a TanStack-Table client grid (sort / paginate / select all in JS); the WAI-ARIA APG "sortable table" pattern is a plain
whose sorted
carries aria-sort and whose header is an in-cell control that re-sorts. For a server-first kit the APG sortable-table pattern wins on (1) a11y (real table semantics, the sort control is a real link that works with JS off) and (5) robustness (the sort and page survive a reload because they are URL state, not in-memory client state). shadcn's grid is rejected: it puts the sort/page truth in the browser, the exact silent-client-state class the pivot removes. So: a real // (composing the table primitive's look),
headers, aria-sort on the active column, the sort affordance as a real (the kit emits the next-sort URL), and the pagination partial for paging. ROWS via a Content slot (data down, the caller owns the cells). The caller renders one @template.data-table.row(...) per data item inside `rows`, each holding @template.data-table.cell blocks (or composes the existing table.row / table.cell primitives). This mirrors the kit's typed Cell switch: the partial owns the chrome + the sort/page contract, the caller owns what a cell shows (a badge, a link, an icon, escaped text). No row data is hardcoded here; it arrives via the slot from the controller's typed model. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG, sortable table): real /// rows (caller owns them; one row per data item). int rowCount = -1 number of data rows; <= 0 renders the empty state. -1 = unknown (trust the slot; never render the empty row). String emptyText = "No data" text of the empty-state row. String caption = null optional
; the active sort column's carries aria-sort="ascending|descending" (others "none"); the sort control is a real so it is keyboard reachable and works with JS off; a visible sort indicator glyph (chevron-up/down for asc/desc, chevrons-up-down for unsorted) is aria-hidden (the aria-sort state is the accessible signal); the optional
is the table's accessible name; the empty state is one row spanning all columns. The pagination partial owns its own nav landmark + aria-current. Params: List headers column header labels, in order (REQUIRED). List sortUrls = null per-column next-sort URL; "" / null = that column is not sortable (no link). Same length / order as `headers`. List sortStates = null per-column active sort: "asc" | "desc" | "" (none). Same length / order as `headers`. null = nothing sorted. Content rows the (accessible name). String ariaLabel = null aria-label when there is no visible caption. int page = 0 current page (1-based); a page < 1 hides the pager. int totalPages = 0 total pages; <= 1 hides the pager. String pageHrefPattern = null printf pattern for a page URL (one %d = the page number); null hides the pager. Forwarded to @template.pagination. String klass = "" extra classes on the . Usage: @@template.data-table( headers = view.headers(), sortUrls = sortUrls, // controller builds the next-sort href per column sortStates= sortStates, // "asc"/"desc"/"" per column from the active Sort caption = "Listings", rowCount = view.rows().size(), page = view.pagination().page(), totalPages= view.pagination().totalPages(), pageHrefPattern = "/admin/listings?page=%d", rows = @@` @@for(var row : view.rows()) @@template.data-table.row(content = @@` @@template.data-table.cell(content = @@`${row.id()}`) `) @@endfor `) --%> @param java.util.List headers @param java.util.List sortUrls = null @param java.util.List sortStates = null @param gg.jte.Content rows @param int rowCount = -1 @param String emptyText = "No data" @param String caption = null @param String ariaLabel = null @param int page = 0 @param int totalPages = 0 @param String pageHrefPattern = null @param String klass = "" !{var colCount = headers.size();} !{var showPager = pageHrefPattern != null && page >= 1 && totalPages > 1;}
@if(caption != null && !caption.isBlank()) @endif @for(int i = 0; i < colCount; i++) !{var label = headers.get(i);} !{var url = (sortUrls != null && i < sortUrls.size()) ? sortUrls.get(i) : null;} !{var sortable = url != null && !url.isBlank();} !{var state = (sortStates != null && i < sortStates.size()) ? sortStates.get(i) : "";} !{var sorted = state != null && (state.equals("asc") || state.equals("desc"));} !{var ariaSort = !sortable ? null : (state.equals("asc") ? "ascending" : (state.equals("desc") ? "descending" : "none"));} @endfor @if(rowCount == 0) @else ${rows} @endif
${caption}
@if(sortable) ${label} @if(state.equals("asc")) @template.icon(name = "chevron-up", size = "0.85em") @elseif(state.equals("desc")) @template.icon(name = "chevron-down", size = "0.85em") @else @template.icon(name = "chevrons-up-down", size = "0.85em") @endif @else ${label} @endif
${emptyText}
@if(showPager)
@template.pagination(current = page, total = totalPages, hrefPattern = pageHrefPattern)
@endif

@import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta

  lievit-ui :: data-table.row - one <tr> in the server-first data table.

  A bottom-bordered, hover-highlighted row; a selected row gets a steadier muted fill via the
  data-state attribute (shadcn parity). The caller renders one of these per data item inside the

  it arrives via the `content` slot from the controller's typed model.

  Params:
    state   - "selected" to mark the row selected, else "" (default).
    id      - optional row id, stamped as data-row-id for the caller's action wiring.
    klass   - extra classes for the <tr>.

--%>
@param String state = ""
@param String id = null
@param String klass = ""
@param gg.jte.Content content
<tr
  data-slot="data-table-row"

A padded, bottom-bordered data cell. Pass scope="row" + as a for a row-header cell (the first column of a row identifying it); the default is a plain . The cell content is the caller's typed projection (escaped text, a badge, a real , an icon), mirroring the kit's typed Cell switch: this partial owns the chrome, the caller owns what the cell shows. Params: align - "left" (default) | "center" | "right". header - true to render a (row-header cell) instead of a . klass - extra classes for the cell. content - the cell's rendered content. --%> @param String align = "left" @param boolean header = false @param String klass = "" @param gg.jte.Content content !{var alignClass = align.equals("right") ? "text-right" : (align.equals("center") ? "text-center" : "text-left");} @if(header) ${content} @else ${content} @endif

@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dialog WIRE template -- an accessible modal dialog whose open state lives server-side in DialogComponent (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/dialog/DialogComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires the `open` action and the backdrop / close button fire `close` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter), so the body + footer are NOT JTE Content slots supplied at call time: they are OWNED markup inside this region. The adopter edits the body + footer regions directly (this is copy-in source), or drives them from @Wire fields / the _instance. That is the server-first equivalent of "children": owned, server-rendered, cannot fail to project. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal) pattern, Radix Dialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - the panel is role="dialog" + aria-modal="true"; - aria-labelledby -> the heading id when a heading is set, else aria-label="Dialog"; - aria-describedby -> the description id when a description is set; - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree. Open-state is SERVER (wire); focus-trap + Escape are a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement (data-lv-dialog hooks): zero open-state in the client, only the focus mechanics. Escape + the backdrop dispatch the `close` action; the close button does too. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Dialog/Modal): div[role=dialog][aria-modal=true] labelled by the heading + described by the description; closed overlay removed from the a11y tree via `hidden`. Params: open boolean modal open state (server-owned) heading String dialog title; wired to aria-labelledby (empty hides the header) description String supporting line; wired to aria-describedby (empty omits it) dismissible boolean when true the backdrop + Escape route to `close` (locked server-side) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, then put YOUR body + footer markup in the owned regions):

Your owned, server-rendered body here.

<%-- Backdrop: a sibling button covering the overlay so a click on the empty area closes (dismissible). Keeping it a real button keeps the close path CSP-clean + keyboard-safe. --%> @if(dismissible) @endif
@if(hasHeading)

${heading}

@if(hasDesc)

${description}

@endif
@endif <%-- Owned body region: replace this with your dialog body markup (server-rendered). --%>

Dialog content.

<%-- Owned footer region: replace this with your action buttons (server-rendered). --%>

@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import io.lievit.wire.FileUploadComponent @import io.lievit.wire.FileUploadComponent.UploadedFile @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui file-upload WIRE template -- a server-rendered dropzone + file list whose upload runs over the wire upload path (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/file-upload/FileUploadComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The dropzone + the selected-file list are server-rendered HTML. The upload itself is the wire runtime's upload directive: the native POSTs the chosen bytes out-of-band to the upload endpoint (runtime features/uploads.ts), which returns a signed temp-file reference; the wire sets the bound `files` field to that reference and re-renders this list. The bytes never ride the snapshot (state-never-code), only the reference does. A remove arms the file name via $set then a confirm action drops it from the server-held list. No . Runtime upload directive: READY (runtime/features/uploads.ts installs l:upload). The adopter wires the SERVER half (the /lievit/upload controller that stores to temp + returns the signed ref); that is application-specific. If you prefer a plain multipart POST instead of the wire upload, swap the l:upload input for inside a
and handle the upload in a controller (the partial recipe). items.name: icon
items.description: Inline Lucide <svg> by name, sized + coloured by --lv-* tokens. Zero JS, CSP-clean. The root PARTIAL: every partial/wire template that needs a glyph composes it via @template.lievit.icon(name=...).

files.content:
@import static it.housetreespa.gest.ui.LievitIcons.body
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui icon partial -- renders an inline Lucide <svg> by name.

  DELIVERY DECISION (scientific, issue #428): inline-per-name, NOT an SVG sprite.
  Compared against MUI (font/SVG components), Radix (per-icon SVG components), shadcn
  (lucide-react, per-icon, tree-shaken) and Web Awesome (<wa-icon>, font/registry):
    (1) tree-shakeability -- inline-per-name wins: only the icons vendored into
        registry/icons/ exist in the body map, so an adopter ships exactly what they use.
        A sprite ships the whole <symbol> sheet (or needs a build to assemble a per-page
        subset) and an extra request / external <use href> with its CSP+cross-doc quirks.
    (2) ergonomics for a JTE adopter -- "add an icon" = drop a .svg in registry/icons/ and
        re-run generate-icon-map.mjs. No sheet to maintain, no <use> id registry.
    (3) robustness -- no <use href>, no external ref, CSP-clean, zero JS.
  The sprite's only edge (repeated-icon byte savings) is marginal in a gestionale and costs
  the copy-in simplicity, so inline-per-name is objectively best here.

  The uniform <svg> wrapper lives HERE once (sized + coloured by --lv-* tokens); the per-icon
  inner body (paths/circles/...) comes from the generated LievitIcons.body(name) map. The
  Lucide SVGs stroke with currentColor, so colour follows the surrounding text colour or any
  Tailwind `text-*` utility / --lv-color-* override on a wrapping element.

  Usage:
    @template.icon(name = "chevron-down")
    @template.icon(name = "trash-2", cssClass = "text-[var(--lv-color-danger)]")
    @template.icon(name = "search", size = "1.25rem")
    @template.icon(name = "settings", label = "Open settings")   // labelled (img role)

  By default the icon is decorative: aria-hidden="true" + focusable="false". Pass a `label`
  to expose it to assistive tech (role="img" + aria-label); then it is NOT aria-hidden.

  Add an icon: drop registry/icons/<name>.svg (any Lucide SVG), run
  `node registry/icons/generate-icon-map.mjs`, commit the regenerated map.
--%>
@param String name
@param String size = "var(--lv-icon-size)"
@param String cssClass = ""
@param String label = null
<svg
  xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  viewBox="0 0 24 24"
  width="${size}"
  height="${size}"
  fill="none"
  stroke="currentColor"
  stroke-width="var(--lv-icon-stroke)"
  stroke-linecap="round"
  stroke-linejoin="round"
  class="lv-icon ${cssClass}"
  role="${label == null ? "presentation" : "img"}"
  aria-hidden="${label == null ? "true" : null}"
  aria-label="${label}"
  focusable="false"
  data-lucide="${name}"
>$unsafe{body(name)}</svg>

items.name: input
items.description: A token-styled single-line native <input>. Carries a real name (POSTs JS-off), binds via l:model on the native input/change events, aria-invalid from the invalid flag. Server-first replacement for the never-form-associated Lit island.

files.content:
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui input partial -- a token-styled single-line native <input>.

  TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The control state lives in the native <input>;
  the partial is the token styling + the focus ring, no client behaviour. The Lit island it
  replaces was never form-associated (the FormData dogfood bug), so the native element is a
  strict improvement: a real `name` that POSTs JS-off, and `l:model` binds the native
  input/change events directly on the element.

  STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn/MUI/Radix were compared. shadcn's Input is the
  native <input> with a border + focus ring driven by tokens; MUI/Radix add a wrapper component
  with JS for floating labels / adornments that the native element does not need for the plain
  case. On a11y and bundle size the bare native control wins outright, so we style the native
  <input> directly (shadcn's exact approach): `appearance` untouched, `aria-invalid` from the
  `invalid` flag (WAI-ARIA), the focus ring is `--lv-ring` raised by `focus-visible`.

  A11y (shadcn / WAI-ARIA model): a real <input> with its own id/name so a <label for> and
  validation bind to it directly. `ariaLabel` provides an accessible name when there is no
  visible label. `invalid` toggles `aria-invalid="true"` + a danger border so assistive tech is
  told about validation state. `describedBy` points at an external hint/error element.

  Params:
    String name                   the input's name attribute (POSTs under this name).
    String id          = name     the input's id (for a <label for>); defaults to name.
    String type        = "text"   native input type (text, search, email, password, number...).
    String value       = null     current value.
    String placeholder = null     placeholder text.
    String ariaLabel   = null     aria-label when there is no visible <label for>.
    String describedBy = null     id of an external hint/error element (aria-describedby).
    String autocomplete = null    native autocomplete hint.
    boolean invalid    = false    danger border + aria-invalid="true".
    boolean disabled   = false    disables + dims the control.
    boolean required   = false    marks the control required.
    boolean readonly   = false    marks the control read-only.
    String model       = null     l:model expression to bind this input to a wire field.
    String cssClass    = ""       extra utility classes on the input.

  Usage:
    @@template.input(name = "nome", type = "text", value = contatto.nome())
    @@template.input(name = "q", type = "search", placeholder = "Cerca...", model = "query")
--%>
@param String name
@param String id = null
@param String type = "text"
@param String value = null
@param String placeholder = null
@param String ariaLabel = null
@param String describedBy = null
@param String autocomplete = null
@param boolean invalid = false
@param boolean disabled = false
@param boolean required = false
@param boolean readonly = false
@param String model = null
@param String cssClass = ""

!{var inputId = (id != null && !id.isBlank()) ? id : name;}

<input
  data-slot="input"
  id="${inputId}"
  name="${name}"
  type="${type}"
  value="${value}"
  placeholder="${placeholder}"
  aria-label="${ariaLabel}"
  aria-describedby="${describedBy}"
  aria-invalid="${invalid ? "true" : null}"
  autocomplete="${autocomplete}"
  disabled="${disabled}"
  required="${required}"
  readonly="${readonly}"
  l:model="${model}"
  class="flex h-[var(--lv-space-10)] w-full min-w-0 rounded-[var(--lv-radius-md)] border border-[var(--lv-color-input)] bg-[var(--lv-color-bg)] px-[var(--lv-space-3)] py-[var(--lv-space-2)] text-[length:var(--lv-text-sm)] text-[var(--lv-color-fg)] shadow-[var(--lv-shadow-xs)] transition-shadow outline-none placeholder:text-[var(--lv-color-muted-fg)] focus-visible:border-[var(--lv-color-ring)] focus-visible:shadow-[var(--lv-ring)] disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 aria-[invalid=true]:border-[var(--lv-color-danger)] ${cssClass}"
  style="font-family:var(--lv-font-sans);"
>

items.name: input-otp
items.description: A segmented one-time-code input rendered server-side: N native single-character <input> slots + a hidden `name` mirror that submits the joined value in a plain POST form (zero wire round-trips mid-entry). The auto-advance / paste-fill / backspace-nav choreography is a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer. Server-first PARTIAL + enhancer (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-input-otp> Lit island. WAI one-time-password-field ARIA (role=group + per-slot Character N of M labels).

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui input-otp partial -- a segmented one-time-code input rendered SERVER-SIDE (ADR-0012). N native single-character s + a hidden `name` mirror; the small auto-advance / paste / backspace-nav behaviour is a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (input-otp.enhancer.ts), NOT inline script. Pairs with registry/jte/input-otp.enhancer.ts. TIER: PARTIAL (+ enhancer). The OTP value is NOT server state mid-entry: there is nothing for the server to decide between keystrokes, so round-tripping each keystroke over the wire would be pure chat for no gain. The slots are server-rendered native inputs; the value is form-submitted (the server binds it at POST via the hidden mirror under `name`), so it is server-bound at the moment that matters. The only client bit is the focus choreography (auto-advance, paste-fill, backspace step-back), which is a tiny typed-TS module the strict CSP accepts (no inline on* handlers). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI one-time-password-field (Radix), the model the dropped Lit island cited: a role="group" of real slots, each aria-label="Character N of M", the per-type inputmode, autocomplete="one-time-code" on the first slot so the platform SMS-autofill fills the whole code, and a visually-hidden mirror so `new FormData(form)` sees the joined value with zero JS. Native inputs beat a custom widget: the platform owns focus, IME, paste and autofill; the enhancer only nudges focus between slots. A11y (WAI one-time-password-field): div[role=group][aria-label] wrapping N inputs each aria-label="Character N of M"; aria-invalid mirrored on the group + slots. Params: name String submit field name; the joined value POSTs under it via the hidden mirror length int number of code slots (default 6) value String prefilled value (default empty); chars distributed across the slots type String "numeric" | "alphanumeric" | "alpha" (default "numeric"); drives inputmode + pattern mask boolean render slots as password fields (default false) disabled boolean disable every slot (default false) invalid boolean error ring + aria-invalid (default false) label String accessible label for the slot group (default "One-time code") Usage: @template.lievit.input-otp(name = "otp", length = 6, type = "numeric") import the enhancer once from your entry module: import "./lievit/input-otp.enhancer"; --%> @param String name @param int length = 6 @param String value = "" @param String type = "numeric" @param boolean mask = false @param boolean disabled = false @param boolean invalid = false @param String label = "One-time code" !{var inputMode = type.equals("numeric") ? "numeric" : "text";} !{var pattern = type.equals("numeric") ? "[0-9]*" : (type.equals("alpha") ? "[a-zA-Z]*" : "[a-zA-Z0-9]*");} !{var slotType = mask ? "password" : "text";}
@for(int i = 0; i < length; i++) !{var ch = i < value.length() ? String.valueOf(value.charAt(i)) : "";} @endfor

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui navigation-menu partial -- a site navigation landmark with real links and optional rich dropdown panels, CSS-only (no JS, no client state). TIER DECISION (why PARTIAL, not WIRE): a navigation menu is, by the ADR's driving rule, "pure display, no client state" once you accept the server-pure overlay. The links are real (the browser navigates, middle-click + open-in-new-tab work); which entry is current is a fact the SERVER already knows (the route being rendered), passed in as `activeKey` -- not client state. There is therefore nothing to hold server-side: no @Wire field, no action, no wire component. A WIRE conversion would round-trip the server just to flip a CSS-revealable panel -- strictly worse. items.name: progress
items.description: A determinate or indeterminate progress bar with WAI-ARIA progressbar semantics; respects prefers-reduced-motion. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui progress partial -- a determinate or indeterminate progress bar. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island computed a width and an ARIA value; both are pure functions of the typed `value`, so the server renders them. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): a token-styled bar with the WAI-ARIA progressbar pattern rather than the native element. The native element's fill cannot be themed by the --lv-* tokens cross-browser (its pseudo-elements vary by engine), and an indeterminate native bar gives no styling hook for the reduced-motion guard; a div+role progressbar (shadcn's Radix Progress approach) themes cleanly and keeps the indeterminate animation in CSS (motion-reduce:animate-none). The track + fill read --lv-color tokens. A11y (WAI-ARIA progressbar): role="progressbar" + aria-valuemin/max + aria-label; for a determinate bar aria-valuenow carries the percent, for indeterminate (value < 0) it is omitted (the indeterminate convention) and the fill animates. Params: int value = -1 0..100 determinate; < 0 => indeterminate. String label = "Progress" accessible name. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the track. Usage: @@template.progress(value = 42, label = "Uploading") @@template.progress() (no value -> indeterminate) --%> @param int value = -1 @param String label = "Progress" @param String cssClass = "" !{var indeterminate = value < 0;} !{var pct = indeterminate ? 0 : Math.min(100, Math.max(0, value));}
@if(indeterminate)
@else
@endif

STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG separator pattern. shadcn/Radix render a
with aria-orientation; the platform has no native horizontal-rule element that also carries orientation, so the role-on-div approach wins (it expresses both orientations and stays decoratable). items.name: sheet
items.description: A modal side panel that slides in from any of the four viewport edges (the richer, shadcn-shaped generalisation of drawer): structured header/description/footer + a built-in Lucide close button. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): open state lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the body + footer are owned template regions, not slots. Replaces the <lv-sheet> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.

@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui sheet WIRE template -- a modal side panel that slides in from any of the four viewport edges (the richer, shadcn-shaped generalisation of drawer) whose open state lives server-side in SheetComponent (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/sheet/SheetComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires `open` and the backdrop / close button fire `close` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter), so the header / body / footer are NOT JTE Content slots supplied at call time: they are OWNED markup inside this region. The adopter edits the body + footer regions directly (this is copy-in source), or drives them from @Wire fields / the _instance. That is the server-first equivalent of "children". STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal) pattern, Radix Dialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - the panel is role="dialog" + aria-modal="true"; - aria-labelledby -> the title id when a heading is set, else aria-label="Sheet"; - aria-describedby -> the description id when a description is set; - the `side` field positions the panel against one edge (right/left/top/bottom); - a built-in close button (showClose) renders the vendored Lucide `x` via @template.icon; - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree. Open-state is SERVER (wire); focus-trap + Escape are a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement (data-lv-sheet hooks). Escape + the backdrop dispatch the `close` action. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Dialog/Modal): div[role=dialog][aria-modal=true] labelled by the title + described by the description; closed overlay removed from the a11y tree via `hidden`. Params: open boolean modal open state (server-owned) side String edge to anchor: "right" | "left" | "top" | "bottom" heading String panel title; wired to aria-labelledby (empty omits the title) description String panel description; wired to aria-describedby (empty omits it) showClose boolean render the top-right close button dismissible boolean when true the backdrop + Escape route to `close` (locked server-side) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, then put YOUR body + footer markup in the owned regions):

Your owned, server-rendered body here.

"right" : side; String panelPos; switch (edge) { case "left" -> panelPos = "inset: 0 auto 0 0; width: min(90vw, 24rem); border-right: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; case "top" -> panelPos = "inset: 0 0 auto 0; max-height: 85vh; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; case "bottom" -> panelPos = "inset: auto 0 0 0; max-height: 85vh; border-top: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; default -> panelPos = "inset: 0 0 0 auto; width: min(90vw, 24rem); border-left: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; } }
@if(dismissible) @endif
@if(showClose) @endif
@if(hasHeading)

${heading}

@endif @if(hasDesc)

${description}

@endif
<%-- Owned body region: replace this with your sheet body markup (server-rendered). --%>

Sheet content.

The open state is server-owned: fire `open` to show it and `close` (backdrop / Escape / close button) to hide it; set `side` to 'right' (default) | 'left' | 'top' | 'bottom', `heading` + `description` for the labelled header, `showClose=false` to hide the close button. Focus-trap + Escape: wire a small typed-TS module keyed off [data-lv-sheet] that traps Tab within [data-lv-sheet-panel] while not [hidden] and dispatches the `close` action on Escape; the open-state itself stays server (wire). For the minimal heading+body edge panel use the drawer wire instead. Adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui switch partial -- a native styled as a sliding switch. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The on/off state lives in a native checkbox: it carries the checkbox role, Space/Enter activation and the checked/disabled semantics for free, and submits its `value` ("on") only when on -- native checkbox semantics the Lit island had to reimplement with role=switch + a manual key handler. `l:model` binds the native change event. We accept the role-switch-vs-role-checkbox trade-off in favour of native semantics + no JS; a checkbox reads as a binary toggle and both patterns are valid for an on/off control (WAI-ARIA). items.name: tabs
items.description: A tab bar + panels where the active tab is server state; only the active panel is shown. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the active id lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-tabs> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG tabs (tablist > tab[aria-selected][aria-controls] + tabpanel[aria-labelledby], roving tabindex). An HTMX content-swap recipe is the lighter alternative for independent panel fragments (see docs).
items.docs: Register TabsComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `tabIds` + `labels` (index-aligned), optional `disabledIds`, as locked server config, then render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.tabs(...)`. Each tab switches with `l:click="$set('active','<id>')"` (the active tab is a scalar, so the $set magic IS the select; no custom action needed); the @LievitRender hook falls back to the first enabled tab and refuses a disabled/unknown active id. The panel BODY per tab is OWNED markup inside the `data-tabs-panel` div: edit it in this copied-in template (there is no <slot>). HTMX alternative: drop the wire and give each tab link an `hx-get` to a controller endpoint returning the panel partial, swapped into the panel container, when the panels are independent server fragments. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/tabs")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: textarea
items.description: A token-styled multi-line native <textarea>. Carries a real name (POSTs JS-off), binds via l:model on the native input/change events, aria-invalid from the invalid flag. Server-first replacement for the never-form-associated Lit island.

files.content:
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui textarea partial -- a token-styled multi-line native <textarea>.

  TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The control state lives in the native <textarea>;
  the partial is the token styling + the focus ring, no client behaviour. The Lit island it
  replaces was never form-associated (the FormData dogfood bug), so the native element is a
  strict improvement: a real `name` that POSTs JS-off, and `l:model` binds the native
  input/change events directly on the element.

  STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's Textarea is the native <textarea> styled with
  the same border + focus ring as Input, vertically resizable. MUI/Radix wrap it for autosize /
  adornments that the plain case does not need. The bare native control wins on a11y + bundle,
  so we style it directly (shadcn's approach): `resize-y`, the `--lv-ring` focus ring raised by
  `focus-visible`, `aria-invalid` from the `invalid` flag (WAI-ARIA).

  A11y (shadcn / WAI-ARIA model): a real <textarea> with its own id/name so a <label for> and
  validation bind to it directly. `ariaLabel` provides an accessible name when there is no
  visible label; `invalid` toggles `aria-invalid="true"` + a danger border; `describedBy` points
  at an external hint/error element.

  Params:
    String name                   the textarea's name attribute (POSTs under this name).
    String id          = name     the textarea's id (for a <label for>); defaults to name.
    String value       = null     current value (rendered as the element's text content).
    String placeholder = null     placeholder text.
    int rows           = 3        visible rows.
    String ariaLabel   = null     aria-label when there is no visible <label for>.
    String describedBy = null     id of an external hint/error element (aria-describedby).
    boolean invalid    = false    danger border + aria-invalid="true".
    boolean disabled   = false    disables + dims the control.
    boolean required   = false    marks the control required.
    boolean readonly   = false    marks the control read-only.
    String model       = null     l:model expression to bind this textarea to a wire field.
    String cssClass    = ""       extra utility classes on the textarea.

  Usage:
    @@template.textarea(name = "note", rows = 5, value = contatto.note())
    @@template.textarea(name = "bio", placeholder = "Raccontaci...", model = "bio")
--%>
@param String name
@param String id = null
@param String value = null
@param String placeholder = null
@param int rows = 3
@param String ariaLabel = null
@param String describedBy = null
@param boolean invalid = false
@param boolean disabled = false
@param boolean required = false
@param boolean readonly = false
@param String model = null
@param String cssClass = ""

!{var areaId = (id != null && !id.isBlank()) ? id : name;}

<textarea
  data-slot="textarea"
  id="${areaId}"
  name="${name}"
  rows="${rows}"
  placeholder="${placeholder}"
  aria-label="${ariaLabel}"
  aria-describedby="${describedBy}"
  aria-invalid="${invalid ? "true" : null}"
  disabled="${disabled}"
  required="${required}"
  readonly="${readonly}"
  l:model="${model}"
  class="flex min-h-[calc(var(--lv-space-10)*1.6)] w-full min-w-0 resize-y rounded-[var(--lv-radius-md)] border border-[var(--lv-color-input)] bg-[var(--lv-color-bg)] px-[var(--lv-space-3)] py-[var(--lv-space-2)] text-[length:var(--lv-text-sm)] text-[var(--lv-color-fg)] shadow-[var(--lv-shadow-xs)] transition-shadow outline-none placeholder:text-[var(--lv-color-muted-fg)] focus-visible:border-[var(--lv-color-ring)] focus-visible:shadow-[var(--lv-ring)] disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 aria-[invalid=true]:border-[var(--lv-color-danger)] ${cssClass}"
  style="font-family:var(--lv-font-sans);"
>${value}</textarea>

items.name: toast
items.description: A transient overlay notification rendered server-side from a flash / notification: severity drives both colour and the ARIA live role (danger/warning are assertive role=alert, info/success are polite role=status) plus a leading Lucide icon. The auto-dismiss timer + dismiss button are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (no wire round-trip: a shown toast needs no server decision). Server-first PARTIAL + enhancer (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-toast> Lit island.

Render the partial from the server's flash / notification (e.g. @if(flash != null) @template.lievit.toast(variant = flash.level(), content = @`${flash.message()}`) @endif) so 'open' is simply 'rendered'. Import the enhancer once from your entry module and call enhanceAllToasts() on load (and after any DOM swap): it starts the auto-dismiss timer (data-toast-duration) and wires the dismiss button. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: toggle-group
items.description: A set of toggle buttons with single- or multiple-selection; the selected value(s) are server state. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the selection lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-toggle-group> Lit island. Radix ToggleGroup a11y: single = radiogroup > radio[aria-checked], multiple = group > button[aria-pressed].
items.docs: Register ToggleGroupComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `values` + `labels` (index-aligned), optional `icons` (index-aligned, '' = none) and `disabledValues`, plus `mode` ('single' | 'multiple') and the whole-group `disabled`, as locked server config; the buttons render themselves. Each item toggles with `l:click="$set('toggleValue','<value>')"`; the @LievitRender hook flips it in the selected set and clears the arm (one click, fully server-side; single mode keeps at most one and allows deselect-to-empty, Radix behaviour). Single mode renders role=radiogroup > radio[aria-checked], multiple renders role=group > button[aria-pressed]. items.name: tooltip
items.description: A floating contextual label, CSS-only; WAI-ARIA tooltip pattern (panel role=tooltip + the trigger's aria-describedby). Revealed by group-hover/group-focus-within. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui tooltip partial -- a floating contextual label, CSS-only (no JS). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island used Floating UI + Lit state to toggle a panel. Per ADR-0012 the server-pure path is CSS: the label text is already in the DOM and revealed by the wrapper's :hover / :focus-within. No positioning JS; the label sits above the trigger in flow (absolute, bottom-full). Escape-to-dismiss (an island nicety needing a listener) is dropped; leaving / blurring the trigger hides it, which satisfies the APG dismissable requirement for a CSS tooltip. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG tooltip pattern rendered with Tailwind group-hover / group-focus-within. The trigger references the label via aria-describedby (the APG contract) so assistive tech announces the description on focus; the visual reveal is the CSS group toggle. A real tooltip role + aria-describedby is what makes this a tooltip rather than a decorative title attribute, so both are kept. A11y (WAI-ARIA tooltip): panel has role="tooltip" + a stable id; the trigger carries aria-describedby pointing at that id, so the label is announced on hover AND on keyboard focus. Params: gg.jte.Content trigger the element the tooltip describes. String content the tooltip label text. String tipId = "lv-tooltip" id linking aria-describedby to the label. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the wrapper. Usage: @@template.tooltip(content = "Save document", tipId = "save-tip", trigger = @@` `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content trigger @param String content @param String tipId = "lv-tooltip" @param String cssClass = "" ${trigger} ${content}
